commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A focused automated repair can fix the stdio cleanup regression; maintainer acceptance of core record/replay and raw recordings remains a separate merge decision.; Needs attention: The diff intentionally adds raw JSON-RPC recording, which is useful but needs explicit maintainer acceptance as a core security boundary.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve stdio cleanup when recording — src/runtime/transport.ts:155-159 (sha=a150f1b3c7f7b089f527784671c67b50b3a990a2)
commands.review_summary:
Adds `mcporter record`/`mcporter replay`, NDJSON record/replay transports with replay response-id rewriting and partial-replay checks, env/daemon handling, docs, and regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: record mode returns a `RecordTransport` wrapper while `closeTransportAndWait()` only finds stdio child handles on the top-level transport. I did not execute a hanging stdio child in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused automated repair can fix the stdio cleanup regression; maintainer acceptance of core record/replay and raw recordings remains a separate merge decision.

target.title: fix(replay): rewrite response ids during replay
target.body:
Follow-up for openclaw/mcporter#175.

This keeps the record/replay feature direction from PR #175 but addresses the actionable review blockers that were preventing merge:

- rewrites replayed JSON-RPC response ids to the active request id, so clients waiting on the current id do not hang
- clears conflicting record/replay env vars when spawning wrapped commands
- bypasses keep-alive daemon fast paths when record/replay mode is active
- truncates a recording session file before the first write in a new recording process
- fails replay close when the recorded workflow was only partially consumed
- preserves child command flags after `--`, so wrapped commands keep flags like `--config`
- changes the README wording from "shareable repros" to "redacted repros"
- adds explicit redaction guidance for raw NDJSON recordings in `docs/record-replay.md`
- removes the release-owned `CHANGELOG.md` edit from this PR

Verification:

- `pnpm exec vitest run tests/record-replay.test.ts tests/record-replay-cli.test.ts tests/cli-daemon-fast-path.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run tests/cli-flag-utils.test.ts tests/record-replay.test.ts tests/record-replay-cli.test.ts tests/cli-daemon-fast-path.test.ts`
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm test` host-side: 122 files passed, 697 tests passed, 1 file skipped, 3 tests skipped

Real behavior proof from an actual record/replay CLI run, using a temporary HOME and temporary local stdio fixture with no real credentials:

```text
RECORD_RUN
{
  "contents": "hello from record replay proof\n"
}
REPLAY_RUN
{
  "contents": "hello from record replay proof\n"
}
RECORDED_LINES=7
RECORDING_SAMPLE
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"$transport/start","_meta":{"dir":"lifecycle","server":"fs-test","ts":"2026-05-31T03:23:24.143Z"}}
{"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-11-25","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"mcporter","version":"0.11.3"}},"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":0,"_meta":{"dir":"send","server":"fs-test","ts":"2026-05-31T03:23:24.145Z"}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"read_text_file","arguments":{"path":"hello.txt"}},"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"_meta":{"dir":"send","server":"fs-test","ts":"2026-05-31T03:23:24.208Z"}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello from record replay proof\n"}],"structuredContent":{"contents":"hello from record replay proof\n"}},"_meta":{"dir":"recv","server":"fs-test","ts":"2026-05-31T03:23:24.210Z"}}
```

Note: the first full `pnpm test` run inside Codex sandbox failed on local `listen EPERM` for loopback and Unix sockets. The same command passed host-side. The sandbox-specific follow-up is tracked separately after this PR merges so host-side socket coverage is not weakened.
